novaDeck.headline: Harness the reaction that fuels the stars
novaDeck.subcopy: Commercial fusion is no longer a decades-away dream — plasma confinement, tritium breeding, and AI-controlled magnetic fields are converging into grid-ready reactors.

modules.label: Plasma
modules.title: Magnetic plasma confinement
modules.description: Superconducting toroidal coils sustain 150 million °C plasma — compressing hydrogen isotopes until fusion ignition crosses the Lawson criterion and energy gain exceeds breakeven.
modules.alt: Plasma confinement chamber with glowing magnetic field visualization

modules.label: Breeder
modules.title: Lithium tritium breeding blankets
modules.description: Neutron-absorbing lithium blankets inside the reactor vessel breed tritium fuel in situ — closing the fuel cycle and eliminating dependence on scarce external isotope supply.
modules.alt: Cross-section diagram of fusion reactor breeding blanket layers

modules.label: Control
modules.title: AI-driven magnetic control
modules.description: Reinforcement learning models predict plasma instabilities 30 milliseconds ahead — adjusting coil currents in real time to suppress disruptions before they quench the reaction.
modules.alt: AI control system monitoring fusion reactor plasma stability

modules.label: Extract
modules.title: High-efficiency heat extraction
modules.description: Liquid metal and helium coolant loops capture 500 MW of fusion thermal output — driving steam turbines at 45% Carnot efficiency for baseload grid injection.
modules.alt: Industrial heat exchanger system in fusion power plant

modules.label: Grid
modules.title: Grid-synchronous power delivery
modules.description: Pulsed fusion output is smoothed through flywheel storage and supercap buffers — delivering continuous AC power that tracks grid frequency within ±0.02 Hz for utility interconnection.
modules.alt: Power grid substation connecting fusion plant to city network
